ucs.title: [LLM] Kimsuky (APT43 / Emerald Sleet) CHM/LNK spear-phish chain — Korean-targeted compiled-help & shortcut payload spawning mshta/PowerShell
ucs.description: Hunts the Kimsuky-signature delivery chain: a victim opens a CHM (compiled HTML help) or LNK file dropped by Outlook/the browser/an archive, and within seconds hh.exe or explorer.exe spawns mshta.exe/powershell.exe/cmd.exe — the canonical execution path used by BabyShark, FlowerPower, and the AppleSeed dropper Kimsuky has run for years against ROK ministries, NK-focused academics, journalists and defectors.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as firstTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_name="*.chm" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.hwp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.hwpx") (Filesystem.process_name="outlook.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="chrome.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="msedge.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="7zfm.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="winrar.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="explorer.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name _time span=1s | rename Filesystem.dest as dest Filesystem.user as user Filesystem.file_name as lure_file Filesystem.process_name as dropper | join type=inner dest [ | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as proc_time values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_name) as child from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="hh.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="outlook.exe") (Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wscript.exe" OR Processes.process_name="cscript.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="curl.exe") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time span=1s | rename Processes.dest as dest Processes.user as user Processes.process as cmdline Processes.parent_process_name as parent Processes.process_name as child ] | eval delta_seconds=proc_time-firstTime | where delta_seconds>=0 AND delta_seconds<=600 | table firstTime proc_time delta_seconds dest user lure_file dropper parent child cmdline | sort - proc_time
ucs.kql:
// Kimsuky / APT43 CHM-or-LNK spear-phish lure → mshta/PowerShell within 10 minutes
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrelationWindow = 10m;
let KimsukyLureFiles = dynamic([".chm",".lnk",".hwp",".hwpx",".docx.lnk",".pdf.lnk"]);
let ArchiveOrMailDroppers = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","bandizip.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let LolBinChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let SuspectLures = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ArchiveOrMailDroppers)
    | where FileName has_any (KimsukyLureFiles)
    | project LureTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              LureFile = FileName, LureFolder = FolderPath, LureSha256 = SHA256,
              DroppedBy = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              UserUpn = InitiatingProcessAccountUpn;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (LolBinChildren)
// hh.exe is the CHM viewer — Kimsuky's CHM payloads spawn mshta from hh.exe
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("hh.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe")
    or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".chm",".lnk",".hwp")
| join kind=inner SuspectLures on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (LureTime .. LureTime + CorrelationWindow)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LureTime)
| project LureTime, ProcTime = Timestamp, DelaySec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LureFile, LureFolder, DroppedBy, LureSha256,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSha256 = SHA256
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Kimsuky / APT43 CHM-or-LNK spear-phish lure → LOLBin child within 10 min
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrelationWindow = 10m;
let KimsukyLureExt = dynamic([".chm",".lnk",".hwp",".hwpx"]);
let ArchiveOrMailDroppers = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","bandizip.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let LolBinChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let SuspectLures = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (ArchiveOrMailDroppers)
    | where TargetFileName has_any (KimsukyLureExt)
    | project LureTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              LureFile = TargetFileName, LureFolder = TargetFilePath,
              LureSha256 = TargetFileSHA256, DroppedBy = ActingProcessName,
              UserUpn = ActorUsername;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where TargetProcessName has_any (LolBinChildren)
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("hh.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe")
    or ParentProcessCommandLine has_any (".chm",".lnk",".hwp")
| join kind=inner SuspectLures on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (LureTime .. LureTime + CorrelationWindow)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LureTime)
| project LureTime, ProcTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          LureFile, LureFolder, DroppedBy, LureSha256,
          Parent = ParentProcessName,
          ParentCmd = ParentProcessCommandLine,
          Child = TargetProcessName,
          ChildCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSha256 = SHA256
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Kimsuky CHM/LNK Spear-Phish — hh.exe or explorer.exe Spawning mshta/PowerShell
id: 4f6c1d2c-8f72-4f7d-9c91-kimsuky00chmlnk
status: experimental
description: Detects the Kimsuky (APT43 / Emerald Sleet / Velvet Chollima) signature spear-phish chain where hh.exe (CHM viewer) or explorer.exe (LNK launch) spawns a LOLBin like mshta.exe or powershell.exe — the execution path used by BabyShark, AppleSeed, and FlowerPower droppers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0094/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt43-north-korea-cybercrime-espionage
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/04/17/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-trustedinstaller-permission/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.001
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  selection_lure_context:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - '.chm'
      - '.lnk'
      - '.hwp'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and (selection_lure_context or 1 of selection_parent) and not filter_user
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate corporate help-file (.chm) usage spawning command shells (rare)
  - IT troubleshooting via Run-dialog LNK shortcuts
level: high
ucs.rationale: Kimsuky has used Korean-language spear-phish with HWP, CHM and LNK lures consistently since at least 2014 (KISA, Mandiant APT43, Microsoft Emerald Sleet, AhnLab BabyShark/AppleSeed reporting). The hh.exe→mshta.exe and explorer.exe→powershell.exe pivot is the actual execution chain observed in BabyShark, FlowerPower, and the AppleSeed first-stage. Anchoring on the file-create-then-LOLBin-spawn with
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Kimsuky (TA427) Outlook/M365 inbox-rule planted by compromised account — auto-forward to Korean free-webmail (Naver, Daum, Hanmail) for dipl
ucs.description: Hunts the Kimsuky post-compromise persistence step where, after credential phishing of a researcher/journalist/diplomat, the actor logs in to Outlook on the Web and creates an inbox rule that silently forwards or redirects mail to attacker-controlled mailboxes on Naver/Daum/Hanmail/Kakao or to a typo-squat Gmail. Proofpoint TA427 reporting and AhnLab/KISA repeatedly call this Kimsuky's preferred long-term mailbox-surveillance technique (T1114.003).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Changes.command) as command values(All_Changes.user) as actor values(All_Changes.src) as src_ip values(All_Changes.user_agent) as user_agent from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where (All_Changes.object_category="mailbox" OR All_Changes.object="InboxRule") (All_Changes.action="created" OR All_Changes.action="modified") (All_Changes.command="*New-InboxRule*" OR All_Changes.command="*Set-InboxRule*" OR All_Changes.command="*UpdateInboxRules*") by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.dest _time span=1m | rename All_Changes.user as user All_Changes.object as rule_name All_Changes.dest as mailbox | rex field=command "(?i)(?:ForwardTo|RedirectTo|ForwardAsAttachmentTo)[^@]*?(?<fwd_addr>[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,})" | where isnotnull(fwd_addr) | eval fwd_domain=lower(mvindex(split(fwd_addr,"@"),1)) | where match(fwd_domain,"(?i)(naver\.com|daum\.net|hanmail\.net|kakao\.com|nate\.com|hotmail\.com|outlook\.kr|gmail\.com|yandex\.|protonmail\.|tutanota\.)") | rex field=command "(?i)Subject[^,]*?(?<rule_subject_filter>(north\s*kor|nuclear|denuke|sanction|defector|reunif|dprk|MOFA|UN\s*panel|fellowship|honorarium|interview|secur))" | eval kimsuky_score=if(isnotnull(rule_subject_filter),3,1) | stats min(firstTime) as firstSeen max(lastTime) as lastSeen values(rule_name) as rule_name values(fwd_addr) as fwd_addr values(fwd_domain) as fwd_domain values(rule_subject_filter) as topic_filter values(src_ip) as src_ip values(user_agent) as user_agent max(kimsuky_score) as score by user mailbox | where score>=1 | sort - score - lastSeen | `security_content_ctime(firstSeen)` | `security_content_ctime(lastSeen)`
ucs.kql:
// Kimsuky / TA427 — inbox rule with auto-forward/redirect to Korean free webmail or look-alike Gmail
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let KimsukyForwardDomains = dynamic([
    "naver.com","daum.net","hanmail.net","kakao.com","nate.com",
    "outlook.kr","hotmail.com","yandex.com","yandex.ru",
    "protonmail.com","tutanota.com","mail.ru"
]);
let KimsukyTopicTokens = dynamic([
    "north korea","dprk","nuclear","denuke","sanction","defector",
    "reunif","mofa","unification","fellowship","honorarium",
    "interview","think tank","38 north","asan","rusi"
]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Application in~ ("Microsoft Exchange Online","Office 365")
| where ActionType in~ (
    "New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","Update-InboxRule",
    "UpdateInboxRules","Enable-InboxRule",
    "Set-Mailbox","Set-MailboxAutoReplyConfiguration"
  )
| extend Raw = parse_json(RawEventData)
| extend Params = Raw.Parameters
| mv-expand Params
| extend ParamName = tostring(Params.Name), ParamValue = tostring(Params.Value)
| summarize ParamMap = make_bag(pack(ParamName, ParamValue))
           by Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId, IPAddress,
              CountryCode, City, ISP, UserAgent, IsAnonymousProxy, ActionType, ObjectName
| extend ForwardTo        = tostring(ParamMap.ForwardTo),
         RedirectTo       = tostring(ParamMap.RedirectTo),
         ForwardAsAttach  = tostring(ParamMap.ForwardAsAttachmentTo),
         SubjectFilter    = tolower(tostring(ParamMap.SubjectContainsWords)),
         BodyFilter       = tolower(tostring(ParamMap.BodyContainsWords)),
         FromFilter       = tolower(tostring(ParamMap.FromAddressContainsWords)),
         RuleName         = tostring(ParamMap.Name)
| extend AnyForward = strcat(ForwardTo, ";", RedirectTo, ";", ForwardAsAttach)
| where isnotempty(ForwardTo) or isnotempty(RedirectTo) or isnotempty(ForwardAsAttach)
| extend FwdAddr = tolower(extract(@"([\w\.\-]+@[\w\.\-]+\.[a-z]{2,})", 1, AnyForward))
| extend FwdDomain = tostring(split(FwdAddr,"@")[1])
| where FwdDomain in~ (KimsukyForwardDomains)
   or FwdAddr matches regex @"(?i)g[m1]ai[l1]\.com$"      // gma1l.com / g1ail.com typosquat
   or FwdAddr matches regex @"(?i)naver-?\d?\.com$"
| extend KimsukyTopicHit = iif(
        SubjectFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens) or BodyFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens) or FromFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens),
        "yes","no")
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId, ActionType, RuleName,
          FwdAddr, FwdDomain, SubjectFilter, BodyFilter, FromFilter,
          KimsukyTopicHit, IPAddress, CountryCode, City, ISP, IsAnonymousProxy, UserAgent
| order by KimsukyTopicHit desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Kimsuky / TA427 — Exchange Online inbox rule forwarding to Korean free webmail / look-alike Gmail
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let KimsukyForwardDomains = dynamic([
    "naver.com","daum.net","hanmail.net","kakao.com","nate.com",
    "outlook.kr","hotmail.com","yandex.com","yandex.ru",
    "protonmail.com","tutanota.com","mail.ru"
]);
let KimsukyTopicTokens = dynamic([
    "north korea","dprk","nuclear","denuke","sanction","defector",
    "reunif","mofa","unification","fellowship","honorarium",
    "interview","think tank","38 north","asan","rusi"
]);
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where OfficeWorkload =~ "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ (
    "New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","Update-InboxRule",
    "UpdateInboxRules","Enable-InboxRule",
    "Set-Mailbox","Set-MailboxAutoReplyConfiguration"
  )
| where ResultStatus =~ "True" or ResultStatus =~ "Succeeded"
| extend P = parse_json(Parameters)
| mv-expand P
| extend PName = tostring(P.Name), PValue = tostring(P.Value)
| summarize Bag = make_bag(pack(PName, PValue))
           by TimeGenerated, UserId, ClientIP, UserAgent, Operation, OfficeObjectId, MailboxOwnerUPN
| extend ForwardTo       = tostring(Bag.ForwardTo),
         RedirectTo      = tostring(Bag.RedirectTo),
         ForwardAsAttach = tostring(Bag.ForwardAsAttachmentTo),
         SubjectFilter   = tolower(tostring(Bag.SubjectContainsWords)),
         BodyFilter      = tolower(tostring(Bag.BodyContainsWords)),
         FromFilter      = tolower(tostring(Bag.FromAddressContainsWords)),
         RuleName        = tostring(Bag.Name)
| extend AnyForward = strcat(ForwardTo, ";", RedirectTo, ";", ForwardAsAttach)
| where isnotempty(ForwardTo) or isnotempty(RedirectTo) or isnotempty(ForwardAsAttach)
| extend FwdAddr   = tolower(extract(@"([\w\.\-]+@[\w\.\-]+\.[a-z]{2,})", 1, AnyForward))
| extend FwdDomain = tostring(split(FwdAddr,"@")[1])
| where FwdDomain in~ (KimsukyForwardDomains)
   or FwdAddr matches regex @"(?i)g[m1]ai[l1]\.com$"
   or FwdAddr matches regex @"(?i)naver-?\d?\.com$"
| extend KimsukyTopicHit = iif(
        SubjectFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens) or BodyFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens) or FromFilter has_any (KimsukyTopicTokens),
        "yes","no")
// Enrich with the sign-in that planted the rule (often from KP/CN/RU IP via residential proxy)
| join kind=leftouter (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online","Outlook Web App","Exchange Online PowerShell")
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, ClientAppUsed
  ) on $left.UserId == $right.UserPrincipalName, $left.ClientIP == $right.IPAddress
| project TimeGenerated, MailboxOwnerUPN, UserId, Operation, RuleName,
          FwdAddr, FwdDomain, SubjectFilter, BodyFilter, FromFilter,
          KimsukyTopicHit, ClientIP, Country, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent
| order by KimsukyTopicHit desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Kimsuky's bread-and-butter post-credential-phish action — documented by Proofpoint TA427 (Apr 2024), Mandiant APT43, KISA and AhnLab — is to set a silent inbox rule on a compromised researcher/diplomat mailbox that forwards copies of mail (typically pre-filtered for keywords like 'DPRK', 'sanctions', 'unification', 'nuclear') to attacker-controlled accounts on Korean free webmail (Naver/Daum/Hanma

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `OfficeObjectId` is not a canonical column of OfficeActivity, SigninLogs. Did you mean `OfficeId`?
